questions.q: What is the primary difference between a PersistentVolume (PV) and a PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC)?
options: A PV is cluster-wide storage provisioned by an administrator, whereas a PVC is a request for storage by a user/pod
A PV is created by the pod, whereas a PVC is created by the administrator
A PV is only for local storage, whereas a PVC is for network storage
A PV can only be mounted read-only, whereas a PVC is read-write
questions.explanation: PersistentVolume (PV) represents actual storage provisioned by the cluster administrator or dynamically by a StorageClass. PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) is a request for that storage by a developer/application pod.
